Back in 2006, 2007, I was MCing my third event ever. And my boss came up to me, I was like, just MCing internally for the company I worked for. He's doing it for free. And my boss comes up to me and he says, Devon, I need you to kill 15 minutes. I'm like, what do you mean? He's like, I need you to go on stage. I need you to waste 15 minutes of time because the next speaker isn't here yet. And 26 years old, I had MCed a total of three events. I was like, what do you want me to do? He says, I don't care, figure it out. I gotta go make a backup plan and he took off. And so I come on stage and I didn't know what to do. And I had 15 seconds to figure it out. And a little voice whispered to me, Devon, just dance with them. And I was like, that is the dumbest idea. The crowd is more of a Led Zeppelin crowd and I was more of an MC Hammer type of, so I was like, this is not going to work. But I didn't have time to second guess what in here was telling me. So I ended up dancing with the crowd. I'm teaching hip hop to a Led Zeppelin crowd. And after the event, they come up to me, they're high-fiving me, they say, Devon, that's the most amount of fun we've ever had in an event. I'm like, you liked that? They're like, yeah, I'm like, why did you like that? They said, because it was authentic. You showed us a piece of you. And even though one of the guys said, even though I don't like hip hop personally, watching you in your element, do you and be you and own yourself was wonderful. And I thank you for that gift. I'm going to give you guys a free download at the very end, just for sticking around. It's going to be awesome. OK, so here's just a little bit about me. I grew up in New Mexico. We used to, that was little Christine playing the piano. Our family used to make about $100 a week. We lived in government housing. We just didn't really have a lot, but I had this really big dream of being a musician. And when I was 17, I recorded my first album and I went door to door selling my music. I don't know if you guys have gone door to door. It is brutal, but it'd be like 150 degrees and I would just knock on people's doors. I'd be like, hey, you want to buy my album? And when I was 25, I moved to Hollywood and I lived in someone's kitchen. So I actually slept in someone's kitchen for a long time and I was really, really broke. But I got my first gig in Hollywood after like 10 years of like living off the dollar menu and like living in Motel 6s. And so I became a full-time musician. It was really cool. And I felt like, you know, things were really working out for me. Things are like going. But then after a while, I kind of just started wanting something different. And so after many years of doing music, I wanted freedom and I wanted to earn from my laptop. I wanted to travel. I wanted to wake up whenever I wanted to do. I wanted to do whatever it is that I wanted to do. And I came across a book that said I could sell my knowledge. And that book said that if you knew something that other people would like to know, you could be a coach and you could start an online business. And I was like, hallelujah. And that book was Expert Secrets. And it was just that one book. I earned multiple two comic club awards just from that book, all the education and gold that was in there. And I learned of who Russell Brunson was. And I realized that I could teach others how to promote their music too. And so I started creating books and courses. And I was kind of just figuring out, I was kind of dabbling, trying to figure it out. And I started personally reaching out to people on Instagram. So I'd be like, hey, are you a musician? I'd reach out to other people on Facebook, hey, are you a musician? And so it's kind of like door-to-door sales. But the problem with that is it's impossible to scale. Because you can only reach out to a certain amount of people because you only have a certain amount of time. So I kept on asking myself, is there a way to get in front of millions of people quickly? Instead of one-on-one selling, could I get in front of groups at a time? And so I became pregnant. Me and my son's dad separated. And I went and I had to live in a garage for a while. I didn't have a lot of money saved up. Just one big thing that happens in your life can really set you back. And so for me, I slept in that garage. I remember being five months pregnant, feeling my son kicking. And I was just so sad. But I was like, you know what? I felt like it was a really dark time in my life. But I also felt like I had found the biggest discovery of my life. I was going to change everything. I was like, oh my gosh, as much as I'm... I was kind of feeling like two different things at the same time. And I held my first webinar in that garage. And I had my son, and that's the only picture I took of myself during this time because I was not happy. It's not like I was like, you know, I'm going to take pictures and videos of me crying. So I took this one photo of me and my son and I was writing my book with the other hand. And I eventually... I was living here in Vegas, but I needed help. And my parents live in New Mexico, so I went to New Mexico. They live surrounded by farmland. They don't have a Wi-Fi connection. Okay, they don't have like cell phone service. They don't have anything like that. And most people would go, hey, there's no way that you can build an online business if you can't get online, right? But I still knew that there was going to be a way. And one day I decided... I had started creating these like little videos on my cell phone. And I was trying to create, you know, sell my books and that sort of thing. And my mom used to come home every day and around 7 o'clock PM, I would take her car and I would drive 10 miles into town where there was Wi-Fi. And I'd upload these little videos. And I started making like a little bit of money and I was like, oh, this is cool. It's working. But then one day I decided to create a funny video. And I went outside and we had a toilet in our backyard because that's what Mexicans do. We just like put like... We had a washer and a dryer and all kinds of other stuff too. But we had the toilet in the backyard and I asked my brother, I was like, brother, sit on the toilet and we're just going to talk about how people are wasting their time sitting on the toilet. And they need to be, you know, promoting their music. And we put it out there. I drove into town. I uploaded it. I just like prayed. And then I went home and of course I didn't have Wi-Fi. So it's not like I could hit refresh and like check to see what I was doing. The next day I go into town to check. I had all these cells, all these views, all these people talking. And within a month I'd spent around $12,000 in ads. Okay, because I didn't have an email list. I didn't have a fan base or anything like that. I didn't have anyone that followed me. I had nothing. I was starting literally from nothing. And I made almost four times what I had spent. And it was wild because I didn't have $12,000 to spend. So some of you guys that are watching are like, yeah, Christine, but I don't have $12,000. Neither did I. What I did was I had $60. And I spent $60 online and I made $100. And so then I spent $100 and I reinvested that and I made $200. And then I took that $200. I just kept reinvesting over and over again. And I saw that it was working. And I was just selling a $19 book. I didn't have this like big, like I take it off or anything like that. So I realized that creating fun video content was the best way to get my offer in front of millions of people. I was just watching. All the people were like, oh my God, I love this ad. I love this. I love you. Have my baby. Like there were just all kinds of crazy stuff, right? It's like, what? I just made a video. It's like wild. And so I've now earned more than 60 million views. I've earned multiple two comic book awards in different businesses. I've done this in e-commerce. I've done this in info products. I have an agency. We've done a lot of stuff. We actually create ads for other businesses now. And using funny and entertaining videos. I've used ads to sell everything from $19 products to $25,000 services. We also create ads for different businesses that sell five and six figure offers. And I say that because funny ads work for all niches. They work for low priced items, high priced items. It works no matter how boring your business is. How do you guys think that your business is boring? How we hear that? Every business is boring unless you sell like fart spray. It's like there's like nothing for like unless you're selling like whoopee cushions, like every business is boring. Okay. But when you think about, you know, you watch like the Super Bowl, right? The biggest companies in the world with the biggest advertising budgets, Advertise using funny ads. It costs $7 million for 30 seconds. Like what? It's crazy, right? So how do you create fun and entertaining videos that sell? What if I'm not fun? What if I'm not funny? What if my business is boring AF, right? I just signed up for car insurance. And everyone's gonna be like, you know, you're not gonna have like, you know, your friends are gonna be like, yo, Bobby, tell everybody that story about the time that you signed up for car insurance. It's hysterical. Like nobody's gonna do that. It's boring. It's everything that you do around the business that makes it funny. And we're gonna get into that. Okay. So here's my simple, funny ad for this is the easiest way to make something funny. Okay. So there's two steps. One, we have to create language that sells. And then we make that funny because our goal is to sell something. Our goal is not to go viral. Our goal is not to make everybody laugh. Our goal is to make our ideal customer laugh. Because we're trying to get them to buy something. Okay. So I'm going to give you guys an example. So first we need to create and we need to share with everyone, but with problem or product sauce. You may have heard this, we want to go away from pain and towards pleasure. So the messaging that we need to put into our ads are what pain point do we solve? What pleasure do we help create for that, for our customers? So example, really simple Tylenol, right? Hey, we get rid of a headache and now you can go live your best life, right? You're out of pain. Easy. You're a marriage coach that helps couples not get divorced. You're a fitness coach that helps people lose weight. That's very, that's language that sells. But to make something funny, okay, we need to have that language first. Okay, you can't just come out and be like, look, I'm covered in poop by my real estate course, right? It can't be that. We have to make an actual pain point of our customers funny. So we're going to exaggerate that pain point. So I'm going to give you an example. So I'm a business coach. One of my businesses, I help business owners learn how to make funny ads. Think of more like leads and sales. Like there wasn't any like crazy like scripts or anything. All we did was say, we just want our customers to look at us and go, he gets me. Like she gets me. She understands what I'm going through. And so anyways, I get a call from a young lady that lived in the Airbnb and she would like maintain it for me. She called me and she's like, this guy pooped in her couch. And I'm like, what? And I'm like waking up and I'm like, what? He pooped in her couch. And I'm like, Vanessa, like what is happening? She's losing it. He pooped in her couch. I'm like, okay, tell me what happened. Apparently this guy decides that the bathroom, which is like 10 feet away, is too far away to walk. So he just like opens the couch and just like sits down and like poops in the couch. How do I segue that into my business? Well, I said, I teach entrepreneurs how to launch businesses online and you'll never have to worry about anybody pooping in your couch. There's all these funny stories that a lot of us have gone through that we can tell online and people start to like you. They start to trust you. They go, I want to work with her. It has nothing to do with anything. I've talked about a guy pooping in my couch. But it's crazy because you start building these really cool relationships with your audience. Now, always include a call to action. So if you notice, I'm always like, hey, comment the word mom and I'll show you how I've built seven for your businesses as a mom, pernure, comment the word start and I'll show you how I built seven for your funnels or whatever. So I always do this. I you can use this to champ. You can use many chat. I like to use this to jump. It's really simple. But one thing that I want to say, there's so many people that get hung up on how to package your product. Like how many of you guys are like, what do I do? Do I do a webinar? Do I do a challenge? Do I do PDF? Like what do I do? And I hear that all the time. Like what's the best day? And so people get hung up on this for forever. Let's talk about Mexican food. How many of you guys like Mexican food? Yeah. OK, so a taco. Let's go to taco to examine what it is. It's a corn tortilla. There's meat in it and there's lettuce and there's cheese. You fold it and it's a taco. But if you roll it, it's a burrito. And then if you leave it flat and you fry it, it's a tostada. And then if you add another tortilla on top, it's a quesadilla. And then if you roll it and you add some sauce, it's an enchilada. Or enchilada, as the gringos would say. It's all the same thing. Like it all works. It all sells. So we get so caught up in like, should I do a webinar? Should I do a challenge? You can test it all. Do whatever you feel like. But it all sells. And eventually, I feel like the Mexicans got it all down. It's like we have a whole restaurant where we got a full menu, but it's like four ingredients. And so just launch something. Don't get caught up on, hey, which one should I launch? And then you take years to launch something. Pick one and get started. So next thing, people only care about the desired results. Like what's in it for me? That's all they care about. And I hear people all the time like, I'm going to give you 400 hours of modules. And I'm like, that's terrible. Like why would you use that in your sales page? Like I don't watch 400 hours of videos. So really what you're thinking of is what's in it for me? So you got to ask yourself, what's in it for my customer? That's all they care about. So if all people care about is the result that they get, how do we make that funny? So here's nothing again, right? We're still talking about pain into pleasure. We exaggerate the desired results that our customers want now. Here's another really funny way. So what do they want? So I have a tortilla chip company. And it's called littlechristies. Littlechristies.com. So we make tortilla chips and we make sauces. And it's in Whole Foods and it's in Sprouts. It's really cool. And I used to sell it out of my trunk. Like I was a drug dealer. I'd like open it and be like, what do you want? I could be stabbed. And then I got it into the little gas stations and then moved into Whole Foods and that sort of thing. So what would a customer want? So now here's the next thing. Now you ask yourself, what does a customer want? They want delicious tortilla chips. Well, how do I exaggerate that to the point that it's just so exaggerated that it's like, come on. Like that would never happen. So here's another way that you do this. You do this with video content. You can actually use other people's videos. If it's paid marking, if you're planning on running it as an ad, ask permission. But if you're just doing organic, you can do it. So here's one is, how to make a funny video just exaggerating the result. And again, I don't say anything in this video and I'm using someone else's video. Using someone else's video. So check it out. I see all these things you guys could take. Like just find someone crying and then you put your product or your coaching program or your whatever. Like they're just so happy. It's so exaggerated that it's just hilarious, right? Another fun way to entertain an audience. And this is just something that's like way up the top. So you exaggerate the result so much that it doesn't even make sense anymore. So we did some ads for Pistia, who is a life coach. And she's awesome and she was promoting a challenge. All right, now I wanna jump into some of the core aspects of selling before I dive into the one to many selling. Because if you start understanding how selling works and how we can weave these things into your presentations, it's gonna change everything for you. All right, so first off, there are three levels of selling. The first level of selling is one-on-one selling. This is one that most people are familiar with, right? Most people in the world are like, oh, you're into sales? How do you do it? Do you do it on the phone? Do you do it door to door? Like how do you do the sales, right? So a lot of people are familiar with one-on-one selling. This is for me the one that I like the least because I'm introverted and I hate talking to people up front. It scares me to death. But when you're starting a business, you're starting something, a lot of times, this is how it starts, there's been one-on-one selling, right? And so for me, basically when I first started my business, when I started calling people on the phone to sell them, I didn't like that very much. So eventually I started hiring people to work for me. I built a sales team, they started calling people, but it was all happening one-on-one. Someone would buy something online, we call them on the phone, we'd sell them something, and that's how we started growing our company initially was through one-on-one sales, right? And so I have some pictures here of other people in our community. This is Jamie Cross. Jamie Cross is someone who started her business also doing one-on-one sales. Jamie sells soap, and so she's built the soap line, she created this amazing, and then she started going initially, she started going door to door. She literally knock on doors at different businesses and tried to sell her soaps. And she got better at doing that, and she was going door to door to door. Then eventually she started in farmers markets, she had her soaps out there, and people would come by and she'd sell them one-on-one one at a time, and she'd do this for a couple years, just making sale after sale one at a time, one at a time. This is Brad and Ryan, these guys are in our circle, and Alice Scripps as well. When they first came into our world, they were doing all their selling one-on-one. They would go to somebody's house, they'd sit down, they'd have dinner with them, they'd make a presentation, and sign them up for their financial services, right? So that's one-on-one selling, that's what most people are familiar with in this world, is one-on-one selling, okay? But one-on-one selling, the problem with that is the bottom level of selling, right? Because you always, one-on-one selling, eventually you cap out, you can't get past a certain, there's always a ceiling, right? Because you only have so many hours in a day, so many people you can talk to, and so you're kind of stuck right there, okay? So if you want to start making more money, you have to evolve from one-on-one selling to I think is a much better thing, which is called one-to-many selling, okay? One-to-many selling, you have an offer, but instead of just talking one person at a time, you have an audience, you're talking to a whole bunch of people at a time, okay? One-to-many selling is what you see a lot of times at seminars and events, right? This is the picture I hear of when I spoke at Grant Cardinals 10X event. I was the one person on stage, I was speaking to 9,000 people in the room, and I had a chance to give a presentation, make an offer, and I spoke to 9,000 people instead of just one-on-one, right? That's one-to-many selling. And some of you guys just heard stories about this experience, it was a really fun one for me, but I want to show you guys kind of the stats of what happened at this live event, okay? So I had a chance to speak on the stage, I had 90 minutes to talk, right? I made an offer at the end, it was a $2,997 offer. There were 9,000 people in the room, like I said, and what's crazy is when it was all said and done, 1,079 people from that room actually bought. Which is crazy, it was awesome. And we actually have a video, how many of you guys want to see what a table rush looks like? Okay, so what a table rush is, is like people get so excited, and they're running to the back of the room to buy the product, they're jumping over the tables, but we're in Mandalay Bay, and so Mandalay Bay is this huge stadium, and it was kind of a nightmare to coordinate the whole thing, but we're like, okay, we have to figure this out, and we're like, how do I get people to run to the back of the room to buy something in this huge stadium? And so we had an idea, like, what if anybody who bought, after they buy, they can run up to the back, you know, up next to the hot dog stand, and get a picture with me, and then that way they have a picture with me ahead of time, right? And so we did the whole thing, did the table rush, people were running to go buy, and then afterwards they all came up, and they stood in this line. And if you look at Mandalay Bay, it's like this big stadium, right? So there's a hallway that goes around the entire thing, and after I spoke, Damon John was speaking, and it was crazy, you're looking at Damon John's, like the stadium's basically empty, and the reason why is because everybody was at Mandalay Bay, wrapped around the sink. The line, the pictures with me, wrapped around Mandalay Bay three times. And who am I? I'm a nobody. No one knew I was five minutes before I got there, right? And then you get Damon John, a legit celebrity on the stage, and everyone's at the hall waiting to get pictures with me. Okay, it's kind of crazy. So we got a little video clip of the table rush, here's some scene and action, because it's one of the coolest things in the world. So check it out. Hey, my name is Russell Brunson, and February 23rd of this year, something special happened. The Grand Cardone put on an event called 10X Growth Con. It was about how to 10X your company in the next 12 months, and I was invited to speak about click funnels, and then make an offer to the audience, selling click funnels as well as a training package. And as I stepped on stage the very first time, I looked at over 9,000 amazing people. And over the next 90 minutes, I gave a presentation, where every second was scripted and choreographed, with one goal, which was to get people to stand up and to run, to sign up for click funnels. The goal was $3 million in sales, and we ended up doing over $3.2 million in that 90 minute window. Russell Brunson spoke to 9,000 people. One out of nine people purchased his product. The reason why I've come, is it's been him right now. You know, he's the best in the world at what he does. Actually, I had to act right away. I drank the pool in, I'm swimming in it. If you don't get this program, you're crazy, very evident. Look at that crowd. What was crazy is we sat there in line, I think it was like five or six hours, I said they're taking picture, after picture, after picture, after picture. It was the most insane experience in the history of my life, it was crazy. Now what was interesting, think about this. We talked about one to one selling, which was great. There's always a ceiling and a cap, right? With one to many, I was thinking about this. I started in the math, I said, okay, if I was doing the same presentation, there's 9,000 people in the room, and if I was to have a one-on-one face-to-face conversation with every single person who was there, like what would it look like, taking that same 90 minute presentation, give it to all 9,000 people, it would have taken 810,000 minutes to have the same impact that I would have had in doing it just once, speaking to 9,000 people. I did the math, if you were to do that 40 hours a week, five hours of working, 40 hours a week, I lined up 9,000 people, I talked to the first person, next person, next person, we're taking 6.49 years to have that much of an impact. I did it in 90 minutes. Is that crazy? Think about just this event right now, 10,000 people around the world who are on this right now, I'm speaking for 90 minutes, think about 10,000 people, it's six and a half years worth of time I'm able to do in the next 90 minutes, which is insane. This is why I love one-on-many, selling one-on-many teaching, because you can have such a broader impact than you ever could doing it one-on-one. And the ceiling, there's still a ceiling on one-on-many, but the ceiling is so much higher, right? It's insane, okay? So getting rich, yes, I've started understanding, if you want us to get really rich in this business, it's all about understanding leverage and speed. And a little while ago, at last inter-circle meeting, I had a chance to interview Mr. Myron Golden, and one of the greatest stage presenters that I've ever seen in my entire life. And we started having this conversation about this, like, how do you grow faster? How do you make more money? Like, what does that look like? And there's this like a 60-second clip from that, that presentation I want to share with you guys today, because if you understand this, it'll give you guys permission to move faster. So check out this clip from Myron Golden. But we talk about the difference of getting rich quick and getting rich slow. Right. So it starts with the question, if you make a million dollars, are you rich? And the people who are watching on YouTube right now, you can just answer the question in the chat, like in the comments. If you make a million dollars, are you rich? That's a good question. And some people would say yes, other people would say no. I would say it depends. How long did it take you to make it? Because if you make $25,000 a year, and you work for 40 years, you've made a million dollars, are you rich? No. What if you make the same million dollars in one year? Yes. But it's the same amount of money. So it's not the amount of money that makes you rich or poor. It's how fast or slow you make it. Wealth denotes speed. So my philosophy is this, either get rich quick or you'll stay broke for the rest of your life. Oh, how's that for a reframe? You've got everyone to post that in the comments, get rich quick or stay broke for the rest of your life. It's insane. I'm making my earn for adding to the presentation. All right, so level one is one to one selling, level two is one to many selling, but there is actually one level that's even better than one to many selling. It's taking your one to many sales presentation, taking it from a one to like to an event and plugging it into a sales funnel. When you do that, that's where you get the mass scale. Think about this, like this is insane. Speaking on stage for 9,000 people, that is insane. It happened for me once. I don't get, I don't want to repeat that every single day. Guess what? Well, that happened, I wasn't there. I was here with you guys. I was hanging out with my kids. I was at wrestling practice, like I was not there and that presentation happened over and over and over and over again, okay? So when you take a one to many sales presentation, you master, you get really good, you plug it into a sales funnel, that's where you get freedom. That's where you can grow, where you can scale and there are no longer any limits, the ceiling disappears on your income potential, okay? And so that's why it's so powerful, okay? So one funnel versus 60 sales guys. Before we launched ClickFunnels, I had a team where we had 60 full time sales guys and I remember, how many of you guys have managed sales teams before? I'll just leave it there. I love sales guys, I'm a sales guy, but we are like the most high maintenance people on the planet. Like we just love money, we're really good at things we do and it's not, anyway. Long story short, we decided to shut down Call Center. I didn't want to manage 60 full time sales guys and like, well how do we keep revenue coming in? So that, well what if we just did the webinar funnel? We just sold the same thing, but on the webinar and then people registered, they watched me get the presentation, it's the same presentation every time, no one's making any crazy claims because I have it all scripted, I have it perfected and then from there, they buy the same thing. And we were able to play 60 full time sales people with one webinar funnel, okay? That's the power, so much less stress, so much easier. I remember when ClickFront was launching, we were like three or four years that I had a chance to go to, Ty Lopez had his Thai mansion back in the day and he invited me to come out there so we went to the Thai Lopez mansion. He sat there and interviewed me for four hours. I was so tired, it was kind of delirious. It was insane. I remember watching it back afterwards, I was like, wow. Anyway, it was awesome. I said, I'm gonna take two people, did the podcast episode, within 24 hours the podcast episode coming through, I had like 60 some odd people who messaged me saying they wanted in. So he had to go to like 20 different banks. This is gonna help you understand how to sell. But the first paradigm shift I want you to understand is that the only thing keeping you from the goals you have right now, from the success you want, the thing you desire the most in your life, the only thing keeping you from that is literally your brain. It's all the things between your ears. I remember when we first started doing funnel hacking live and we started doing these live events, right? And people would come and everyone was talking about funnels and like, and try and traffic and we'd talk about all the tactics of it, right? And people go home and a lot of people wouldn't have success. I'm so frustrated. Okay, that's the game plan. So again, then this is the metaphor. Obviously I'm not a brain doctor, there are people way smarter than me. If you guys understand this metaphor, you have everything you need, they understand about your brain to actually be successful, okay? Do you guys cool if I simplify it? All right, good, so here's the metaphor. So there are three core parts about your brain we're gonna be talking about, and I'm gonna break this down and teach you guys the parts of the brain with my friends right here, these stuffed animals. Now I'm doing this because we do a kid event every couple of years called Unlock the Secrets, and the first time I taught this, I taught this to a kid event. I was like, all these adults know this stuff, I'm gonna teach to the kids, and I brought my friends along, I taught it to the kids, and the kids were like, this is amazing, they finally got it, and all the parents were like, we finally understand it. So I know you guys don't need the elephants for your kids, but just, hopefully it'll help for everybody, okay? And again, I'm showing you guys for two reasons, number one, for yourself, and number two, to show you how to persuade people afterwards. Okay, so I'm gonna walk through the three parts. The very first part of the brain I wanna talk about is your conscious mind. This is the thing that me and you were talking about right now, our conscious mind, right? And in the metaphor, there are two parts of your conscious mind, and we call these the monkeys. Buddha called the conscious mind the monkey mind, is always chattering back and forth, talking back and forth, right? And all of us, we have these conversations happening all day long, right? All of us are talking to ourselves all the time. Right now you guys are asking questions, like what's Rolfs doing, what's he talking about? Is this real, is this not right? This is consciously happening, right? Now our monkey mind, I had two people in my monkey mind. One of them is emotion, this seems like a very emotional monkey, right? Here's our emotional one, and this is our logical, smart one, okay? We have emotion, we have logic, emotion, logic, okay? Now some of you guys, everyone here is driven, like one of these monkeys is usually louder for each of you guys. As a kid, traditionally, the emotional monkey is usually a lot louder, he's the one that wins, right? As you become more adult, all times logical one comes bigger, but we still use both these when we're talking, we're having decisions, and emotional logic are fighting back and forth all the time, having conversations, talking, all whole bunch of stuff like that, okay? I didn't bring him here, but the kid event, I brought out a box of Twinkies, and I went out in the audience, and I showed the Twinkies that I said, I had a parent and a child, I said, who wants a Twinkie? And the kid's like, I want a Twinkie! I was like, hey, what am I, and I was like, that's emotional, give me a Twinkie! And I said, parent, do you want a Twinkie? And they're like, no, I'm like, why not? And they're like, well, it's because it's got high calories, it's got high fructose corn syrup, it's gonna make me feel sluggish, and the logical mind, and I was like, how interesting, right? Every time we have a decision, two monkeys look at like, ooh, I want that emotionally, but what about this, what about, this looks so good, I'm so hungry, look at the calories, we can't eat that, come on, and chattering back and forth, right? How many of us make that same argument every time you got to eat? If you guys been to the Cheesecake Factory, you open it, it was like 8,000 pages, so they have the menu item next to the calories, so you look at the menu, you're like, that looks amazing, and the calories is like 4,500 calories for one slice of cheesecake, like, what? But you're like, but it's gonna be so good, and this is gonna take us two weeks to blur it and stop, but come on, this is the thing, right? This happens all the time, this happens also when we're having conversations, like trying to decide about business, do we do the things we not, right? Two comic book, I wanna win a two comic book, or this is gonna be amazing, right? Like, that's gonna be hard work, you failed in the past, you're gonna fail again, don't do it, don't do it, like, come on, I want one, and we're chattering back and forth, right? So this is your conscious mind, and it's arguing all the time. How many of you have this conversation right now with yourself, okay? How many of you guys is the emotional one you're driver typically? Raise your hand on my emotional buyers? How many of you guys are more logical and illogical buyers? Okay, we got both sides, so this is your conscious mind, there's the metaphor for conscious mind, okay? All right, so conscious mind, couple things, again, it's motion and the logic. Now, the thing that I understand about the conscious mind, the conscious mind speaks in questions, it's always going back and forth and back and forth. You know it's your self conscious mind when you're asking yourself a question, oh, should I do this, should I do red headline, a blue headline, should I go out to eat with these people, should I not go out, and it's always having these constant questions, okay? Now, the other thing that's interesting about the conscious mind, is the conscious mind can only get information from five different sources, okay? It gets into these senses, so it's through sight, sound, taste, smell, and touch, okay? So when we have a problem we're trying to attack with something, our conscious mind can only get information through these different things, okay? A lot of times if I can't solve a problem by looking at something, reading something, smelling, if I can't do those five senses with five inputs, if I can't get information through that, I can't solve the problem, which is why we'll talk about subconscious mind later. Subconscious mind work completely different to help you solve problems. A lot of times that's why when you're trying to solve a problem you're like, I'm trying to solve a problem, I can't figure it out, I can't figure it out, I can't get more information, I'm studying, I'm researching, and then you get frustrated and you walk away, you're not on a walk or something, or maybe you're in the shower the next morning, or you're in the shower, and all of a sudden it pops and you're like, here's the answer, it's because you weren't consciously doing it. Your conscious mind can only get the information through the five senses, and if you can't find those five senses, you cannot consciously find the answer. You just break out of that, anyway, that's a whole chapter of another book I may be writing someday that you're gonna love. Anyway, again, the best part of the conscious mind that we have to understand, the best part of the conscious mind is it gives us the ability to actually choose what do we want, okay? That's what it has, so I want this thing, we consciously choose that thing, and the monkeys are fighting back for it, no, I want this, I want to win two problems on the board, I want to lose weight, I want to run a marathon, I want to choose the thing we want to do, right? So that's the coolest thing about the conscious mind, and that brings us back to the polling hills quote, I shared on day number one, right? Like we consciously get to pick the purpose we want, okay? There's one quality which one must possess to win, and that's definitely it's a purpose, which is the knowledge one wants and burning desire to do it, okay? We consciously get to pick, I want this thing, I want to go to state title, right? That was my conscious thing, I want to make a million dollars a year, I want to start a business, I want to build a community, I want to buy a library, whatever the weird things you guys have, right? I want to buy an old book, like we consciously get to choose, okay? So we have to figure out what is our definite goal, what's our purpose? Now I'm gonna share a couple of quotes from this guy here, how many of you guys know what Earl Nightingale is? Okay, if you know Earl Nightingale, he is one of the greats in the self-help world. Think about, see the Napoleon Hill like wrote books, when Napoleon, Earl Nightingale came on, it was like, things are shifted from books to like spoken words, to radio, so he was on radio every single day, talking about personal development, sharing ideas, sharing thoughts and stuff like that. In fact, I remember my dad had never heard of like Napoleon Hill, these people, my dad like, oh I know Earl Nightingale, we used to listen to him all the time on the radio. He was on the radio personality, and there's a really cool story, that's this picture of this gold record, I'll tell you a quick story about this. This is actually at their, Napoleon Hill, or at Nightingale Code's office, he had a chance to go there, and they brought out, this is the gold record. And so the story behind this is, Earl Nightingale was, he owned an insurance company, and he had a bunch of sales guys who worked for him, and then he was a radio host, right? And he was about to go on like a three month long sabbatical, he had a boat that he bought, he was gonna go like sail the ocean, right? So he's about to leave, and his assistants like, hey, before you leave, the sales guys are gonna have no training for three months while you're gone, do you mind recording something I can give them, so they can listen to it every week, and we can train them and keep them all motivated? He's like, sure, so he sat down the morning, wrote out an outline, got on his recorder, and he recorded this audio, it's called The Stranger's Secret, he recorded it, he took it to his assistant, gave it to her, she went and she put it on a record, and next week sales meeting while he was gone on a boat, gave it to them, and they all listened to the record, and they're like, this is really, this is the best thing Earl Everett, this is amazing, right? And that's where it's supposed to end, but then all the sales guys took the records home, they printed like 40 of my things, each man had their own record, and they started like playing for their friends, and their family members, and everyone else wanted a copy, they started calling the office and asking his assistant, hey, do you have any more records, I want one, and she's like, ah, so she went and printed a couple more, sent a couple of the friends and stuff, and she's like, okay, get back to work, you guys, yourselves, we will go sell stuff for Earl while he's gone, right, and then a couple more people got it, kept spreading, more people started calling, he started going viral in a really weird era where there was no computers, but it started going viral, and then somebody at Earl's radio station got the record, this is amazing, so when Earl was normally live on air, they played the record for the entire world to hear, and people heard the record, the strangers, they started freaking out, they started calling, and she started getting all these phone calls at the office, and she's like, what do I do? And so basically while Earl was gone, all the sales guys stopped selling, and they built like a shop where they were literally just printing records like crazy, and shipping out, printing records, shipping it out, and she's like, I'm in so much trouble, when he gets back, Earl's gonna fire me, I'm in so much trouble, fast forward out three months later, Earl and I go, pulls up in a ship to the harbor, he gets off the ship and there's this huge, like hundreds and hundreds of people there, and he's like, what do I do? Am I under arrest? And he walked to the harbor master, he's like, am I under arrest? What's gonna happen? And he goes, you should probably call your assistant. So he calls his assistant, and she's like, I'm so sorry, we're shipping these things out, he's going crazy, I don't know what to do, like should I stop? And he's like, keep shipping them, I'll see you guys tomorrow, whatever. And so he's shipping them, he shows up the place, they find out they had sold, I can't remember, like 100,000 plus records while he's gone three months, and it became the very first record that ever went, a spoken word record that went platinum. And on top of that, when Earl jumped on the boat, he was like 100,000 air, when he landed on the boat, he was a millionaire. Isn't that crazy? From The Stranger's Secret. So The Stranger's Secret, if you haven't heard it before, it's like a 45 minute talk, it is insane, but I got two pieces, my two favorite parts of The Stranger's Secret, I want to share with you guys today. The first one is about the conscious mind is picking the thing we actually want. So here's a clip from The Stranger's Secret with Earl Nightingale. Have you ever wondered why so many men work so hard and honestly without ever achieving anything in particular? And others don't seem to work hard and yet seem to get everything? They seem to have the magic touch, you've heard them say that about someone, everything he touches turns to gold. And have you ever noticed that a man who becomes successful tends to continue to become successful? And on the other hand, have you noticed how a man who's a failure tends to continue to fail? Well, it's because of goals. Some of us have goals, some don't. People with goals succeed because they know where they're going. It's that simple. Think of a ship leaving a harbor and think of it with a complete voyage mapped out and planned. The captain and crew know exactly where it's going and how long it'll take. It has a definite goal. Now 9,999 times out of 10,000, it will get to where it started out to get. Now let's take another ship, just like the first, only does not put a crew on it or a captain at the helm. Let's give it no aiming point, no goal, no destination, we just start the engines and let it go. I think you'll agree with me that if it gets out of the harbor at all, it will either sink or wind up on some deserted beach at derelict. It can't go anyplace because it has no destination and no guidance and it's the same with a human being. Now let's get back to the strangest secret in the world, the story that I wanted to tell you today. Why do men with goals succeed in life and men without them fail? Well, let me tell you something which, if you really understand it, will alter your life immediately. If you understand completely what I'm going to tell you from this moment on, your life will never be the same again. You will suddenly find that good luck just seems to be attracted to you. The things you want just seem to fall in line and from now on you won't have the problems, the worries, the annoying lump of anxiety that perhaps you've experienced before. Doubt, fear, well, there'll be things of the past. Here's the key to success and the key to failure. We become what we think about. Now let me say that again. We become what we think about. Throughout all history, the great wise men and teachers, philosophers and prophets have disagreed with one another on many different things. It's only on this one point that they are in complete and unanimous agreement. Listen to what Marcus Aurelius, the great Roman emperor, said, a man's life is what his thoughts make of it. The Israeli said this, everything comes if a man will only wait. I've brought myself by long meditation to the conviction that a human being with a subtle purpose must accomplish it and that nothing can resist a will that will stake even existence for its fulfillment. Ralph Waldo Emerson said this, a man is what he thinks about all day long. William James said, the greatest discovery of my generation is that human beings can alter their lives by altering their attitudes of mind. And he also said, we need only in cold blood act as if the thing in question were real and it will become infallibly real by growing into such a connection with our life that it will become real. It will become so knit with habit and emotion that our interest in it will be those which characterize belief. Okay. That awesome? Earl Maingale, let's go. So I'm going to show you guys an X clip from Earl nightingale. Also from the strangest secret talking about our subconscious mind. Now, how does it work? Why do we become what we think about? Well, I'll tell you how it works as far as we know. Now to do this, I want to tell you about a situation that parallels the human mind. Suppose a farmer has some land and it's good for the land. Now the land gives the farmer a choice. He may plant in that land, whatever he chooses, the land doesn't care. It's up to the farmer to make the decision. Now remember, we're comparing the human mind with the land because the mind, like the land, doesn't care what you plant in it. It will return what you plant, but it doesn't care what you plant. Now let's say that the farmer has two seeds in his hand. One is a seed of corn. The other is nightshade. A deadly poison. He digs two little holes in the earth and he plants both seeds. One corn, the other nightshade. He covers up the holes, waters and takes care of the land and what will happen. Invariably, the land will return what's planted, as it's written in the Bible, as he's so, so shall you reap. Now remember, the land doesn't care. It'll return poison in just as wonderful abundance as it will corn. So, up come the two plants. One corn, one poison. Now the human mind is far more fertile, far more incredible and mysterious than the land, but it works the same way. It doesn't care what we plant. Success, failure. A concrete worthwhile goal or confusion. Misunderstanding, fear, anxiety and so on. But what we plant, it must return to us. You see, the human mind is the last great unexplored continent on earth. It contains riches beyond our wildest dreams. It will return anything we want to plant. So that's how the subconscious mind works, right? It's planting these seeds. And in one of Elsie Lincoln Benedict's books, she talks about this as well. People ask, well, if I have all these negative habits, what do I do? And the first thing you do is you stop watering it. You stop nursing it, right? If you have a bad habit, you just got to stop feeding it. We keep feeding the same habits over and over and over again. We got to stop feeding it and then start creating the hypnotic rhythm, creating the habits that are positive, that are successful. Okay? You have to understand, I believe, I've talked to some of my friends about this, but I believe that when all is said and done, who we are as a person, who do we become? What we're talking about, I'm on this journey to become something. Who we become is literally the record of our subconscious mind. This is who we become. By the time we die, all the habits, all the patterns, all the things like your subconscious mind is who you actually are when all is said and done. And so for us, the lifelong journey is to be the ones who are training this thing. This is how we become something different. We consciously decide what we want and then we train this. And over time, if we train it correctly, that's how you become something different, how you become someone more. So you look at this, what is our job? Our job consciously is to stand guard of our mind. Because if you're not careful, things are going to bypass it. We've got the monkeys here with their spears blocking things. Because you have to be careful what you allow into your mind. Because if you listen to really, really bad music, you listen over and over again, that builds these hypnotic rhythms. You're watching bad movies, builds hypnotic rhythm. You're following people who aren't positive, like builds this hypnotic rhythm. It's hard to get rid of those things. So we have to consciously stand as a guard in front of this gate and protect ourselves. What are we going to allow into our subconscious mind? Like, oh, this doesn't affect me. Playing bad video games doesn't affect me. What are you watching bad movies that doesn't affect me? It affects all of us. It's literally building these hypnotic rhythms over and over and over again. And it's becoming who we actually are. So the monkeys stand as sensuals protecting it. That's the big key. All right. Next thing here. So we have our conscious mind with the monkeys, our subconscious which is the elephant. And there's one more part of the mind that actually matters to me. And I'm trying to be successful in anything. And the third one here is the instinctive mind or fear. And my friend the lizard here is going to be a demonstrate fear for us. So this is fear. Now, fear is interesting because this part of your brain, if you do, you study the whole science of it, like it's really fascinating because fear doesn't tell you what just happened in a situation, right? It just tells you how to feel about it. Okay. And fear's job is to protect you. So it's actually like a really good thing. It's something that's built from God into our bodies, into our minds to protect us. But the problem is that most of us don't actually need to be protected all the time. Maybe back thousands of years ago when there were like mammoths chasing you in lines, like you needed to fear like, like freak you out so you could run. But nowadays, like we live comfortably. Like for the most part, this is one of the things that's destroying most of our lives. It's trying to protect us. Okay. Now, what's interesting, if you look at this, fear, you see when a baby's born, there's only two fears it has, right? You guys know what they are? One is the fear of loud noises. I think that's right here. And then there's fear of falling, right? Those are the two fears that you were born with. Okay. Only two fears. No other fears you're born with. And then what happens is over time, you learn all the other fears. Okay. You're a two-year-old kid, you're walking around and your mom sees a spider and she freaks out, oh, there's a spider. And all of a sudden your body's like, oh, okay, spiders are like, if we see a spider, we should freak out, right? All of a sudden like you learn to fear spiders and then to learn to fear of whatever else and then it starts learning all these other fears. Okay. And then a fear of the body and the goal of the lizard is to protect you from physical harm, right? So start learning these things. Okay. So those are all of the different, the different fears we have of our body. Okay. Now the second side, though, and this is the one that's really tricky. This is one that we have to deal with as entrepreneurs, also as people who are trying to persuade and influence other people is understanding the fears of the ego. This is one that's harder for most people. Okay. And Tony Robbins says, you look at all the fears people have of boils down to two things. Number one is that you're not enough. And number two, if you're not enough, then you're not going to be loved. These are the fears of the ego. These fears are not real. Okay. These fears are fantasy, but they're, you're, this lizard here is trying to protect you from everything. Like, what if I go and try that diet and I fail? I'm not going to be enough. I'm not enough. I'm not going to be loved. So it's trying to protect you. I don't do that. Don't try to think. What if I'm going to try to make some money? What if you fail? Well, if you fail, you're not good enough. And if you're not good enough, you're not going to be loved. Right. And so it's trying to protect you from everything because it does not want your ego to have to go through that kind of pain. Right. And your customers the same way, by the way, like you're trying to make a presentation to them, they're freaking out that all the spheres happen because they are afraid they're not going to be enough. If they fail at the thing and if they're not going to be enough, they're not going to be loved. Okay. And so it all ties down to status. People's fear of losing their status. Okay. Now fear is also interesting. We talked about how the monkeys, they speak in questions, the subconscious mind, the elephant speaks in stories and fear speaks in absolutes. Okay. You know, it's fear because it's not going to ask you a question. It's going to be telling you something. You're not good enough. You're not pretty enough. You're too old. You're too young. You're smart enough for that. There's no way you're going to be able to do that. I mean, it's what Star Wars only sits dealing absolutes. It's the same thing. Fear only deals absolutes. It's the thing chirping back of my telling you, not good enough. You're strong enough. You're not worthy. So if you hear something coming at something telling you, it is not your conscious mind, it's not your subconscious mind. It's literally fear just trying to protect you. It's not real. Okay. Click to learn to turn fear off. And we start looking at these conversations. We start realizing all these voices in our head that are happening all the time. We look at that. This is what all comes down to you, right? Our conscious mind speaks in questions. Subconscious mind speaks in stories and our instinctive mind speaks in absolutes. Okay. Now our job for ourselves, for us to be successful in anything in life is very, very simple. Okay. Our job is number one is to calm the lizard. Dude, it's going to be okay. You just relax. I know it's going to be scary. It's okay. Calm the lizard. Okay. Number two, you gotta guide the elephant. This is how you have success. This is what we're going to do. We're going to do this. That's our success in life. Okay. Are the monkeys and elephants that help a little bit? Okay. All right. Very cool. Now again, I'm telling you guys this for two reasons. Like I could do a whole book or a whole event just on psychology. It'd be successful in life. And this would be the foundation we go from there. But for the tents and purposes of what we're doing today is how do we use this for something else? How do we use this to persuade and sell and influence? Right. And so again, I'm showing this for two reasons. All you guys are getting in business because you have something you believe you have a result, you have a product, you have a service you believe can change someone's life. Right. So your job is to help them get out of their way. How do you shut down their fear? How do you train them? How do you tell the stories? Right. They get them to actually move. Okay. And so we call this subconscious selling. So when I am on stage talking, here's me. This is Russell. When I'm talking to all of you guys here at home, what's interesting is that most of you guys think I'm talking to your monkeys. Cause I'm talking, we're having a conversation like, oh, me and Russell are talking. We're hanging out. But the reality is I'm just doing this to distract these guys because I have to bypass them because if I'm going to actually get you to change your life, actually to pursue things that have success in anything life, I have to get past your conscious mind. I got to talk to the elephant. Okay. I have to bypass it and get to the elephant because this is the only thing that actually matters. If I can convince him that you're capable of it, then you can move. Otherwise, if I don't get him in his buy-in, you're not going to move. No matter how much you want to win or two column club, or how much weight you want to lose, how much, whatever your goal is, if this guy's not bought in, it will not work. Okay. And the third thing is that you're not fear. So the fear is not fighting you away, keeping you away from doing the thing that you're called to do. Okay. That's the subconscious selling is all about. Okay. So again, the subconscious mind, it speaks in stories. So we have to learn how to tell stories because when I tell a story, I'm bypassing your conscious mind. I'm speaking directly to this guy right here. Now this guy right here, he knows a lot of stories. He's been hearing stories for your entire life and he remembers these stories and they're stored in here. So what happens is a situation comes up in your life and he's like, oh, I have a story for that. Boom. It finds the closest story that it knows that it believes is true and it plugs in that story. So something comes, he's like, Hey, here's the thing. You can, you should go after a start a business when two column club award. It's like, what story do I have? Nope. He tried to business three years ago. It failed. He felt like an idiot. Right. Yeah. Yeah. He warned me. I was going to try and I wasn't going to be good enough and he was right. I failed. I'm not good enough. People didn't let me. I took that story. So it pops the story. Boom. Here's the story. Like, ah, conscious. They want to do it, but there's a story that doesn't work for me. Without even knowing it is happening so fast and you have a feeling and that feeling is a positive negative six, your stomach excited. So first thing we want to talk about is how do you tell stories? I said, this is the key. Okay. We keep thinking that the reason that people are going to buy is because we're going to logically convince them. People do not buy things logically. Okay. They're buying them emotionally, but it's coming back to here, right? So the epiphany bridge story. So you're a normal person. You have some experience, right? You start studying and learning and geeking out and you get the worst thing possible. Yeah. This is the motion over here. You have this emotional experience. You start getting logic, all these logical arguments, right? You start taking these things. You start storing all the facts and the figures and all sorts of stuff. And then when you see a prospect walking by, you get so excited that you spew out all the logical arguments. You think that's what people need to buy, but it's not how you bought. We call this techno babble. Okay. And so what we got to do is kill techno babble. If you want to persuade somebody, you have to remember what was the emotional experience you went through. You have to go back in time and remember what was the thing that you experienced that gave you that change? This is the story you tell someone. You tell them the story and you tell it in a way that gets them to have the exact same epiphany that you had. You're just telling, here's the story. I believe you're telling the story. You don't just tell them a quick story. Like, yeah, I had a story like, I had a website, I wasn't making money, and then I put an upsell in and then people bought. So I love funnels. Like that's not a story. You tell a story in a way that emotionally connects people with you so that they have the same epiphany that you had. I'm not telling them my epiphany. I'm telling my story and then they have their own epiphany. That's how you tell a story, right? And then after they have their own epiphany, then they'll go on this journey by themselves and they'll logically convince themselves they'll do the study and the facts and the figures, all sorts of stuff. It's not so much fun. So, okay. First one. So this is the false belief a lot of your customers have, a lot of my customers have is that they get to the offering and you're like, hey, it's going to cost, you know, $25,000 or $50,000 and then your customer's like, oh, I can't afford that. If I know that's coming, one of my favorite false belief story closes that I use earlier in the content of my training is the rich dad, poor dad close. Okay. So I'm going to go into character. Let me become rustle. I'm going to the rich dad, poor dad close. Okay. Right. How many of you guys were the book rich dad, poor dad? Okay. Okay. When I first read the book, it was crazy because I was, I was young. My wife and I just got married. My dad gave me that book and I remember it sat on my shelf for like three or four months and I didn't, I didn't ever want to read it. So I didn't read it. And then eventually, I'm one night, I couldn't fall asleep. So I grabbed the book. I started reading it. I read the entire rich dad, poor dad in one night. I was like, this is insane. I read the book and I remember there's something that Robert Kiyosaki said that changed my life forever. Okay. He said, if you read the book, he's talked about rich dad and his poor dad. So he's got two different dads. One was rich, one is poor. And he said, because he had a chance to see how rich people thought and poor people thought, he said, it's really fascinating. He's like, poor people look at something that they really, really want. They see that thing like, oh, that's really cool. I wish I could afford that. I can't afford that. He's now rich people. They look at the exact same thing. Instead of saying, I can't afford that. They look at things, say, how can I afford that? What happens when you say, how can I afford that? Also in your mind starts shifting, starts thinking, well, I could do this. I could do this. I could do this. It changes everything. Right. So anytime I have a situation on my life where I look at something and I, I know it's more expensive than I, than I have. Like I want a new house, I want a new car, I want to invest in a training program, whatever, and I can't afford it. I don't look at that and say, oh, I can't afford that. Instead of stuff, okay, cool. How can I afford that? I start thinking, it opens up my mind. In fact, the very first time I wanted to get a training program, I remember, um, it was a thousand dollar course. It was my very first internet marketing course ever. And I remember my wife and I, uh, we were broke as a joke. Uh, we had no money and I remember I didn't invest anything. It was a thousand dollars. I didn't have a thousand dollars. So what I didn't say to actually pay for it is I was like, how can I afford that? How can I afford that? Luckily I had to trigger it to that point. How can I afford that? How can I afford that? I started thinking, I started thinking. All of a sudden I was like, looked outside and there were leaves falling on the ground. I was like, there's leaves outside. What if I go and get a rake and I rake up some leaves and people give me money for raking up leaves? I said, that'd be amazing. So I bought a tarp, bought a rake, wanted to knock on the door. Hey, can I rake your leaves? Cool. And I rake the guys leaves, took them out, threw them away. Guy gave me a hundred bucks. I'm like, I'm one tenth of the way there. Knock another door, hey, can I rake your beliefs? Boom. Had knocked next door, knocked another door. Within 10 lawns, I had a thousand dollars, bought the course, changed my life. It all came because I didn't think, I can't afford that. I said, how can I afford that? That little shift changed everything. So when you are looking at anything you want in life, you cannot think like a broke person. If you think of a broke person, you're going to be broke forever. Never look something I can't afford that. You look at this and say, how can I afford that? How can I afford that? It'll change everything. In fact, I had a student who came to our program. Okay. And they wanted to buy us a certification program, certifying people on how to actually be a funnel builder. And they came in and they found the price point and it was really expensive. High five figures for this price point. I can't afford that. And then as they stopped and said, wait, how can I afford that? And they realized, well, I can't afford to buy this program right now. But if I buy the program, I can help other people. What if I went to somebody who I could build a funnel for and said, Hey, I don't have money to buy this thing, but if you invest for me, I'll build your funds for free, all the ability to learn skillset. And then I'll go do the thing. So they had somebody else to buy it for them. They then, then they started working for that person for free to pay it back. And then they got the skillset. And after that, they were able to use that to make a whole bunch of money afterwards. Okay. So all comes down to understanding where to learn from Rich Dad, poor dad. Okay. Don't think like a poor person. Think like someone who's rich. Never say I can't afford that. Always say, how can I afford that? Boom. You guys got that?
