And you're probably thinking, you have an exciting business, you have something that everybody wants, right? And you'd be right. I teach people bookkeeping. So you'd be right. It's exciting. OK, it's bookkeeping. We're talking about bookkeeping here. And over the next 25 minutes or so, I'm going to show you the simple process that I use to scale my business and how all of you can do the same and why the most important goal that you make for yourselves today and you should be making goals for yourself is not based on how much you make, but based on how much you spend. OK, so let's walk in. Get into it. OK, let's get into my progression. All right. So as you know, this amazing community gives out awards. You know, when you hit certain levels of revenue, right? You know, there's a two comma club award. You know, there's the X award. And then there's the mother of all awards. I earned this first award in the year 2020. OK, I earned this next award in the year 2023. And I earned this last award in the year 2024. OK, and if you look at this, you're like, OK, well, it took you three years to go from here to here, but it only took you one year to go from here to here. But the distance between these two awards is a heck of a lot bigger than the distance between these two awards. So you're probably thinking, what happened? What was he doing here? What was he doing here? And it has to do with the F word. And I'm just going to I'm not going to write the whole thing just yet. OK, I don't want to trigger anybody. I don't want to offend anybody. OK, but it has to do with the F word. And that's why I'm here to talk to you today. All right. Now, when this first award came, what did I do? Comes in the mail. You get so excited. You crack that thing open. And a lot of us, we grab the award and we tell our wives or a significant other, hey, take pictures, right? Take pictures of this because guess what I'm going to do now? I've proven some success with what I've done. Now I can show other people how to do what I've done. Now I can be a coach to other coaches. Right? I've got one successful thing. Now I can create another successful thing. All right. And so that's what I started to do during these three years. I started putting that together. And during that process that was very challenging, my wife was on the stage of the award. My wife was on the internet. She looked at some of my competitors in the bookkeeping space and she said, you know, these guys are doing really well. They're crushing it out there. There are some people charging $12,000 for something that I was charging $2,000 for. They are crushing it. I think there's more to this because here I was trying to get this other thing going and my bookkeeping training practice was just plon alone. OK, it wasn't growing at all. I thought I had two vehicles for success. I had the bookkeeping training and then now I was going to train coaches and two is better than one. If this is doing well, I can get that doing well and I'll make twice as much money and have twice as much impact, right? Well, that's where the F word comes in. So if you're easily offended, you know, turn away, but it has to happen. Focus. OK, this is the new F word. This is the new F word. And this is what allowed me to have this kind of success. When I had those two things going at the same time, I thought I was going to multiply my income, but what happened was I divided my focus. All right. And I'm here to tell you that every single person here and everybody watching online is sitting on a hundred million dollar business idea, but most of us will never get there because we get distracted. It's very easy to get distracted as entrepreneurs. We have all kinds of opportunities hitting us at all times. All right. And this is one of the biggest killers of businesses of all sizes. Whether you're talking about entrepreneurs just getting started or major corporations, this lack of focus and distraction. And you have to look no further than Facebook. Right. Facebook started what was called the metaverse. We all know that wasn't very successful. It was outside of their core business. Right. They do social media and they do it well. And they lost hundreds of millions of dollars on that. And what did they do? They went back and focused on social media. And if you own Facebook stock, you're very happy about that because they're at all time highs right now and contrast that with Amazon. Amazon sold books and only books for four years. And in the world of entrepreneurship, that's like a thousand years. OK, four years. They could have sold all kinds of different things like they do now. And they only sold books. They didn't start selling anything other than books until they hit about a billion dollars in revenue. OK. And so there are plenty of books about this. I won't go any deeper into it than that. But there are plenty of books out there. And if you like a book that covers this topic really well, it's changed my life. It's called The One Thing by Gary Keller. And the TLDR for you is you basically focus on one thing, the biggest thing in your business that if you accomplish it, everything else you need to do in your business becomes either easier or insignificant to the point that you don't have to worry about it at all. OK. And focus is hard. We all know that. And that's why there are so many books on this topic because it means saying no to all these other top opportunities that sound great. And we all know when we're uninformed optimism, we might have heard that term when we see a brand new opportunity, it sounds great because we don't know everything about it yet. Everything sounds great until we actually start doing it. OK. So how did I use this concept of focus to create such income and impact? Well, it's called The One Funnel. The One Funnel. And this One Funnel will simplify your business, it'll simplify your life and allow your business to reach its maximum potential in the shortest amount of time if you just give it. OK. So let's get into it. OK. Let's talk about a traditional funnel. You start with traffic. You know, and we bring people, as we know, into our selling mechanism. Our demonstration, whatever that may be, webinar, challenge. Let's use a challenge. As an example. All right. And then we sell them something. OK. We have our offer. OK. This is a pretty simple funnel. Again, you can use a webinar, you can use a VSL, you can use any of those things. OK. And this is typically how it starts. And for most people, they do a couple of tweaks here and they get this thing working and it starts to working. And the first thing they say and ask themselves is, this is working, this is having success. What else can I create and sell? And that can have success too. All right. And so before you know it, they create a bunch of other offers, a bunch of funnels. They've got 12 different funnels, 12 different offers. They've got upsells and bumps and cross sells and downsells and all these different things and nothing is working particularly well because they haven't focused on anything well enough for it to grow to its potential. OK. And what's more challenging? Getting one funnel and offer to work or 12. It results in confused customers. They see mixed messaging. They don't know who you help, what you sell. And consider the fact that in your business, you're fulfilling on 12 different offers. So there's total chaos. Who here is guilty of this? Lots of funnels, lots of different offers at home. Yeah, I've seen some hands. It's called shiny objects, syndrome, woman, the red dress, whatever you want to call it, but you're not alone because not only do I suffer from this or I did, but the most well known marketers on the planet suffer from the same thing. You see their stuff and you're like, what do you do anymore? Who do they help? OK, so I started focusing on the one funnel and I want to go ahead and dive into it and how you can use focus to squeeze as much out of this as humanly possible. OK, and it starts like a traditional funnel. All right, you take this funnel and the first place you focus is the offer. OK, you've got to pick the right offer. All of your businesses have a wide variety of offers, whether it's the stuff at the bottom of your value ladder, like the free stuff, the low ticket stuff, you got mid ticket, you got high ticket, you got all that stuff. And you've got to pick the right offer for this to work. OK, and the right offer is right in the middle of your value ladder, like your mid ticket product. OK, so think about something in the one K. To 10 K range. OK, that's typically going to be the best offer. Why is that? Well. With your mid ticket offers, typically you have the biggest discrepancy between price, how much you're charging and fulfillment costs. OK, so you're able to charge a lot for it, but you're not spending a lot of time fulfilling the offer and you want that biggest discrepancy that typically happens in the mid ticket. OK, you cannot scale to six figures and ad spend with the wrong offer. Anything lower than mid ticket, you got to sell way too many to move the needle. Anything higher than that, 25 K, your ultra high ticket stuff. It's probably going to cost more in terms of fulfillment. Too much of your time. You want something where you can sell this and deliver it on it. On an assembly line. OK, so that's what you want to do. So once you got the offer dialed in and this thing is working, instead of creating something new, which is what most people do at this point, how can we focus and squeeze more out of this? And there's three steps to transforming this funnel or any funnel because you will have funnels that qualify for this into a one funnel. And step number one is you focus on the non buyers. Non buyers. As much as we'd like to say 100 percent of people else of our challenge or webinars are going to buy our offers, we know that they don't affect most of them. Don't the vast majority of them don't. And so what do we do with those people? Well, most people just send them to like an email nurture sequence. You know, send a bunch of emails. Hopefully they'll buy something. And they just don't or they just very few of them do. OK, and some people don't even follow up with email. So but that's not even the most important part of this process. The first thing you're going to do when it comes to non buyers, some people who don't take your offer is you're going to create fewer non buyers. Said another way, you want to get more people to buy your offer off your challenge or webinar. So what you're going to do is unlike most people who just they throw up a webinar challenge and they see how it converts, it converts, OK, they're like, OK, let's run a bunch of traffic to it. What you're going to do is you're going to apply tons of focus and iterate, basically run your webinar challenge live over and over and over again and tweak and change the little things that increase conversions. OK, you focus obsessively here. You can take, let's say you're at like 10 percent conversion rate on a webinar challenge, you can take that easily to 15 percent just by changing things, just by changing your stories. We just heard how important stories are changing your closes. Try those different things and increase the conversion rate. This is kind of the linchpin of the whole thing. You need this to be converting as high as possible. OK, so you spend a lot of time there. OK, and now if you execute this properly, not only will you get more people into your offer, but you will get more people who are non buyers to still want to buy something from you. They want something from you. So what do you do with those people? Those non buyers you send down here into a low ticket community. OK, you sent to a low ticket community like a forty seven dollar a month. Maybe a ninety seven dollar a month low ticket community. They want to buy something from you. And what do we do with this low ticket community? Well, you know, with your offer here, you're promising to help them reach a goal, reach an outcome. And with any outcome, you've got steps that need to be taken to achieve that goal. Right. So in this community, you're going to help them achieve the first two to three quick wins, help them do the first two to three of those things. OK, this allows people to experience a little bit of success with this. Get that quick win, get excited about it and get further ingrained in this process. OK, the more you can get people in motion doing something, the more that they are likely to continue. OK. And so what's the benefit of this? You might be saying, well, we're making a little bit of extra money, which is great. But you're actually doing two things. OK, the extra money that you're making, it's great. But you're increasing the what's called the enterprise value of your business. Remember, funnels and offers are great. We have legitimate businesses. These are businesses as well. And when we structure our businesses, we want to grow them as big as we can. And potentially to exit at some point. And the most valuable businesses are those with recurring revenue. OK, so this helps accomplish that. You can grow this to six figures in monthly revenue, which is great. And it increases the the enterprise value of your your business, which is great. But the most important thing this thing does is it gets people moving so that you can then send them back into this high ticket offer. That's what you really want this for. You want to squeeze as much out of this as possible. And as the name implies, a one funnel is all about one funnel, one offer, and it keeps feeding itself. OK, so you're constantly pushing people back to your main offer. OK, so what do you do with the people who didn't buy this and you didn't buy this? Well, we're going to go figure out another product for them. We're just going to create this community. And this one's going to be free. And what's this going to include in it? Well, this one includes like the first two to three steps that they need to take. This one is just going to include step one. The first step that they need to take, you get them to join a free community. They get that first quick win. And then what happens? They get upsold into this community where they continue their journey and then they again get sold back into your main offer. OK, when you do this, you have essentially squeezed all of the juice out of this. You've got this converting as high as possible and you're converting as many non buyers as humanly possible. OK, and you can easily double your conversion rates. If your challenge is converting now at 15, this can easily move to 30. Just by doing that, by focusing on these non buyers and doing more than just sending them some emails. OK, so now what can you do? Well, you've got this thing working, you've got this thing converting. Now you can start creating other offers and products. Right. I think you know the answer to that. No, no, we're going to treat this thing like it played baseball in the late 90s, early 2000s and inject it full of steroids. OK, that's what we're going to do. We're going to keep focusing on this thing. So what do you do when you have something that's working well? You run it more often. You run it more often. You cannot reach your full potential as a business owner if you're doing this piece live, OK, webinars, challenges, you can't do it. Right. So the key here is to automate this. And I'm going to give you some tips on how to automate this properly. OK, so if you're doing a challenge once a month, where are my challenge people? Once a month, once a quarter. OK, we got some challenges. Where are my webinar people at? You're doing webinars, you're doing them once a week. If you're doing challenges, you're doing it every week. You're doing challenge every week now. If you're doing a webinar once a week, you're doing it every day. Or you're doing it five times a day. OK, but what's the biggest concern when you automate your webinar or challenges? We're afraid of what going down? Conversions, OK, conversions, that's the biggest fear. Well, what if there was a way to automate this where conversions didn't move? Where they stayed exactly the same without you having to be present? Would you guys be interested in hearing about how to do that? OK, because doing it live, that does increase conversions. So how do we preserve that live feeling? How do we preserve that live feeling so that we can keep conversions high and people perceive it to be live? Well, you know, there's lots of different software you can use to try to automate things. And I work with kind of an older demographic. And what are the chances of, you know, Marge from Iowa downloading some weird strange tool on her computer to have to navigate this process? Not very likely. There is a platform out there that everybody on the planet is familiar with. OK, when it comes to live webinars, live meetings, live events. What's that platform? It's Zoom. But up until today, the problem with running automated stuff on Zoom is you needed a human to participate. You might be able to click play on something and kind of stand there, but you have to end it. You've got to share your screen. You've got to do all kinds of different different things. But you've got to use Zoom. OK, you have to do it. So I'm going to put you up on a new feature that Zoom has that's going to change everything when it comes to automating for you. OK, it's called Simulive. Let's type. I just write automate with Simulive. Simulive. OK, this is a new feature on Zoom. And what it will allow you to do is upload a recording. So if it's your webinar, your challenge recordings, you upload them. Then you schedule a webinar. You tell it when to start. You select what video recording you wanted to play. And at the start time, without a human being being present, the meeting will start playing the video. And when that video is over, the meeting will end with no human being necessary. Is that cool? Is that cool? This is a game changer and you and your team can still be in the chat. OK, you and your team can be in the chat, still interacting with people, maintaining that live atmosphere. That's how you automate your challenge or webinar and keep conversions just as high as they've ever been. OK, so that's step two. That's step two. We've got our offer down. Then we focused on our non buyers. We've really iterated this and made it great. And then we've automated things. Step three of turning this into a one funnel is, well, kind of this piece of it. This thing is running well. It's running often. How do we shove more people into this? OK, and here's what you do. All right, you've got maybe a webinar funnel. Maybe this is like a challenge. So you got like a webinar funnel. OK, maybe you've got like a book funnel. And you're saying, Bill, I thought you said one fun. I thought you said one fun. Yeah, normally when people build these other funnels, they're selling all kinds of different products, all kinds of different offers. But we've optimized this funnel to convert as high as humanly possible. So, for example, your webinar, you've got traffic going to your webinar and the call to action at the end of the webinar is not another offer, which is what most people do. The call to action is to get them to do the thing we know converts them. You go straight to the challenge. You're not selling any offers on this webinar. You're driving traffic straight to the thing that you focused on, but you know converts as highly as humanly possible. Same thing with the book. They go buy a book, they go to an OTO page, a one time offer. And guess what they get asked to do? Get in my challenge. All right, so all funnels, when you're working with a one funnel, lead to your one funnel. They do not create other offers. They do not sell anything else. OK, so you've got webinars, you've got challenges, you've got books, but everything feeds into the one thing that works really, really well. And before I move on, I do want to mention, because we've talked about the value ladder, you could, once people buy this offer, ascend into like an ultra high ticket, maybe you've got like a 25 K offer, you could do that. Right. And a lot of us do do that. We have masterminds. We've got things like that. You could do that, but you won't. You will not do that. You will not want to do that. When you create like this ultra high ticket offer, generally speaking, the fulfillment costs, time, money are high enough that you'll just say, you know, if this is a 5 K offer, if this is a five, let me just run five more people through this and then I don't have to deliver on anything else because I've already got this on an assembly line, so you just won't do it. OK, so this is a one funnel. Again, to recap, starts like a regular funnel. You focus on the offer, you get this challenge converting as highly as possible or your webinar and you just keep tweaking and testing your stories and your calls to action and your your hooks, all of that stuff. And then you deal with your non buyers, you automate it, and then every other funnel you create drives to your one funnel. OK, and this is a one funnel. And this is what happens when you just focus and you don't chase all of these other things and other opportunities that might present themselves. So now those of you paying attention are like, OK, that's great. You talked about driving more people into this. What about this piece right here? Traffic. Where do I get traffic from for a one funnel? Where does it come from? Well, you could do organic, right? Organic free stuff. You could do paid ads. There is one right answer and only one right answer with a one funnel. Paid ads, only paid ads. Frankly, I'm sick of the debate. What's better, organic or paid? I'm sick of it. Every serious business on the planet. Advertises. Everyone advertises. If you don't advertise, you're not serious about your business. You're not serious about it growing as big as it can get, as fast as it can get. And as a result, you'll never reach your potential. And why is that? Two things, reach and speed. With ads, as we know, we can reach as many people as we want. Just raise the budget. Speed. You could probably publish an ad before I leave this stage. I've got three minutes left. You could probably publish an ad before I leave this stage. When you've got this one funnel that converts so highly, why would you take it slow? Why would you not cram as much into this as possible? That's why some of our favorite marketers out there, we see their ads over and over and over again. A lot of you have probably seen my ads because I keep shoving them in front of you over and over. And that's what our favorite marketers do. Success leaves clues. What is the use in saying, hey, I made a hundred thousand dollars off my webinar this month with no ad spend, all profit? Because what I'm hearing is that, well, you could have made five hundred K with a hundred and fifty thousand dollar ad spend and walked away with three hundred and fifty thousand dollars in profit. Right. And then consider, again, we're running businesses here. And when you're in business, you've got threats. You've got threats to your business all the time. You've got competitors. You've got a changing marketplace. Your offer is working now. There's nothing that says it will two years from now. So you want to maximize on the opportunity that you have right away as quickly as possible. And so this one funnel is exactly how I've been able to spend up to one hundred and fifty thousand dollars per day on ads. And it's going to convert so well that you will convert at three X, four X, five X, return on ad spend. You hear a lot of people that are spending a hundred dollars a day, five hundred dollars a day and like, hey, I'm getting a five X return on ad spend. Well, everybody here knows who's done paid ads knows that when you try to scale, that return goes down, not when you do it this, not when you do it this way, not when you've got this thing converting as highly as possible. And when you're able to spend six figures a day, and this is why I am talking about this and why Russell has talked about this over and over again, is that you know that you're pushing your funnel and your offer to the absolute limit, you're growing as quickly as possible, as possible and as big as possible. OK, learning how to advertise is the cheat code. It absolutely is. Most people who don't do it are afraid, you know, hey, I'm going to lose money. I'm going to, you know, it's not going to work. I don't know how to do it. Guess what? Business is hard. OK, you have figured other things out in your business, probably bigger problems than that, figure that one out and it'll change your life. OK, so now what I encourage you to do when you're making goals for your business is not to set a revenue goal. Don't say no. Hey, I want to make a million dollars this year. I want to be five million dollars next month. Set an ad spend goal. Right. When you send an ad spend goal, you say, I want to spend a hundred thousand dollars a month or a hundred thousand dollars a week or a hundred thousand dollars a day. You know that not only is the return there because you're converting so highly, but you also know and you can sleep comfortably knowing that you are doing everything you possibly can in your business to grow it as big as possible as quickly as possible. Plus, when you pay for ads, you can put them on credit cards. Right. You will never pay for travel again. You'll always fly first class. So maybe credit card points is the real goal here. I'd love to hear you say, hey, Bill, last month I made seven million credit card points. I did eight figures in credit card points. That's the new flex. OK, that is how you optimize your business. That is how you use focus to do that. And that's how you can make more than you've ever dreamed possible. You've got less hair than I have. You just took off your glasses. I don't know what your name is, but I'll wave to you. He's got a beautiful right head tilt. So I looked at that as a coach 10 years in and I thought to myself, one of that has an effect on me. He is in Las Vegas. Does a head tilt all the way to Sweden make a difference? Oh, absolutely. Because if one of you were to tell me, hey, my hamster died this weekend, I would intuitively do this. Oh, because it's a show of empathy. So what he's doing and what all of you are doing, he's got your head tilted left or right. You're literally sitting there and you're going, oh, when I'm standing here. Not only that, but that gentleman there. I wish I knew your name. He's got another skill going on where I can see all the way from here. He's nodding affirmatively now and then as I speak. And the question then is, does that impact me all the way from Las Vegas to Sweden? And absolutely does because it shows that he is affirmative into what I'm saying. And that encourages me to speak even more. Right? Have you met the person who hasn't got these two skills? They're literally doing this when they listen to you. Yeah. Uh huh. Mm hmm. Mm hmm. Yeah. Which is a big difference compared to somebody who does this. So as a coach, what I did is I took these two skills and I gave them to someone who didn't have them, which is about 40 percent of everyone we studied does not have these. And I assure you, there will be a bunch of you who hasn't got them either and you are missing out in life. Because what happened as soon as they gave I gave them these two skills, they said to me, David, you're mad. So you want me to tilt my head and you want me to nod affirmatively when I listen to people. That's going to be strange. It's going to be so artificial. But I said, well, the thing with all new skills and new golf swing and new paddle smash, it doesn't matter. Everything feels artificial to begin with, but all the skills you have, you've learned. So they started using these two skills. They got back to me after a while and they said, this is mind blowing, David. People speak more to me now. They open up to me more now. I sell more now. Hey, that's not rocket science. It shows that you care for God's sake. You may have cared before, but now you actually show it. So I had a look at these two skills and I thought to myself, wonder if there are more skills. So I embarked on the adventure of studying speakers and presenters all over the world until I'd found every single one. It took me seven years. And as I said, I'm never ever going to do this again. Seven years studying five thousand speakers. I found two hundred and thirty six skills, not two, two hundred and thirty six. Bit too complex. So I boiled it down to one hundred and ten core skills. That means that all of you, I'm looking at you in the cameras, I'm looking you sitting on the stage there in Las Vegas, all of you has the potential to use these hundred and ten core skills. What they look like is this. Are you ready? Because this is going to be quite a shock for some of you. You may have read books on body language. You may have read books on voice. You may have gone to coaching, to public speaking sessions. You've never seen anything like this. I love science. I love periodic tables. Will bring this one out in full screen email. Who's my technician here? And it looks like this. These are the hundred and ten core skills that all of you have the potential to use when communicating. Now, isn't that mind blowing? I'll put it in the middle and it symbolizes your product, your service, which you want to sell, which will you want other people to buy. OK, are we clear on that? That gives you two options. Either you stand on this side of the chair, you shoot from the hip, you hope for the best, you trust that your public speaking skills and presentation skills are fair. And sometimes it works, doesn't it? Sometimes it doesn't. But look at this. Option number two is that you're standing on this side of the chair and you know exactly what to do in every instance of time. You know that by taking a step forward, you gain attention. You know that by sitting down. You enter a different psychological level. You know that by slowing down your pace, you increase clarity. And you know that every single time you lower your voice, you grab even more attention. And at the end of the day, you're absolutely certain. That if you pause, the room goes silent. things are important but you need to have an average pace as well if your average pace is too slow you lose them as well so pace take that with you as skill number eight skill 21 is pauses and what's interesting with this is that I think skill 21 is one of those skills that people are least good at why is that well maybe it's because as soon as you pause you get absolute attention from absolutely everyone and everything you're in the spotlight so what do people do instead what do people do well one strategy is just speaking really quickly and just skipping pauses all together which is just tiresome and you can't digest what's being said so what do people do well they pretend to be sheep don't they so they go I had a client once who came to me and he asked me to coach him and I said right first thing we got to get rid of is your filler sounds and he went like do I have a lot of filler sounds yes you do I said and listen to this this was almost torture for him what I did is I asked my assistant to edit out every filler sound from one of his videos okay so he had a 30 minute video edit out every single filler sound into a different audio file and when I put him in the middle of the room I put on the speakers and then I said sit down I want you to listen for this for three minutes because that is the amount of filler sounds you've had for your entire presentation and he looks at me like I'm mad or something I put him down in the chair I put on the filler sound audio and it starts going and he comes running out of the room after 90 second he goes I can't take it I can't take it but you like we had an agreement you have to go in it's night another 90 seconds and it goes in he comes out so motivated he never ever wants to do a filler sound again because the thing with filler sounds is you use the most amount of filler sounds when you are uncertain insecure nervous afraid when you're thinking and when you're lying that's when we use the highest concentration of filler sounds which means that if you use filler sounds in your pitch in your presentation you leave it up to the other person to figure out why you're using them are you using them because you're nervous lying or thinking they don't know it's very dangerous to leave simple things like that up to the person listening so I want to demonstrate this for you now I'm going to give you a classical rhetorical proverb I'm going to do it without pauses to begin with I've listened to this did you know that every single decision you've ever taken in your life and you will take for the personal space for business people leading social scientist Albert T. Hall defined personal space in 1954 as the region surrounding a person which they regard as psychologically theirs invasion of personal space can often lead to discomfort or anxiety on the part of the victim I love that giant in the end right please use this when you present by simply moving forwards to people moving forwards to people you grab people's attention in video it's all about leaning these skills saying that I want to give you one tip when it comes to virtual presentation specifically and it it comes down to one skill which is eye contact and particularly called amplified eye contact which is skill 75 amplified eye contact this is a bit of a bonus for you but it's so important so I saw Russell talking about the brain before and in the brain you have a neuropeptide called oxytocin I think you've heard about it we can get it when we hug and when we kiss yeah a lot of you nodding yeah okay oxytocin is produced via eye contact we know that so if I meet a person we got eye contact oxytocin has the opportunity to be released if we feel a trust for each other the cool thing is this there's a country neighboring Sweden which is called Finland in Finland they did a study during covid which showed that oxytocin was produced or similar effects to oxytocin was produced via camera and eye contact via camera and eye contact which is then so scared when I look at where you but you can't you can't shut it off because it's right here and it's pulsing and it's beating and it's moving and if you don't move on it the only choice you have is die or keep drinking and ignoring this thing inside of you if you're drawn here to cook funnels it's highly likely that you are one of the 1% crazies welcome crazy oh so my life is My life has changed. In so many ways, it's just, it's amazing to think. I was global head of marketing for the largest foreign currency exchange company in the world, and I was in charge of 28 countries. I was flying internationally twice a month. I was a corporate tax expert suit and all the whole gamut. We were the top home buyers in South Florida. We built a massive real estate business in the early 2000s. I was meeting like 30,000 people a month to my website through these recipes, and I thought, well, I might as well monetize this. And so I got certified as a personal trainer and started writing ebooks. I was picking up trash in Columbus, Ohio for $6.25 an hour. So I drove a trash truck during the day, and then I sold insurance and investments at night. How weird is that? I was a guy who did really well in school, studied hard, I graduated three years, and I got the consulting job that was like the fancy job that we're supposed to get. In 2006, my mom said, Alex, you know, you guys are doing a lot in real estate. Have you ever thought about diversifying like all your investments, all your works there? After I had my son, I was a stay-at-home mom, and he gained 70 pounds during my pregnancy, so my stomach was like way out here. I was 65 pounds overweight. I was like, had total food addiction, all this stuff. And, you know, one Christmas, I was with my family, and I was like going for like my sixth snickerdoodle cookie, because that's like my grandma's famous for her snickerdoodle cookies. And I remember Nick telling her mom, if something was going to happen to us in real estate that would hurt us, it would be front page news. I had been selling ebooks on my website and doing OK. And then I went through a divorce, but then I just started really drinking. I was going for like my sixth one, and she like reached out and she like smacked my hand. She was like, Kailin, do you really think you need that cookie? And I was like, mama for as a school teacher and qualified for food stamps. And that's kind of a low blow after your whole life. You're told you need to go to college so that you can have financial security. You're on food stamps. We were on Medicaid. I had made $25,000 as a father for the year before. And then I got audited by the IRS in 2010. And I got the businesses. He got all the cash and had Alamone, your child support. I just was very empty. I definitely felt the golden handcuffs. It was like a rock top moment. Like we have a house. I have children. I got everything I was supposed to. I can't just be like, I want to like make balloon animals. I feel like this is really it. I'm already a senior manager in this tax practice, but I'll quit. Oh, it was terrifying. In 2007 in South Florida, the market in the areas we had properties went down over 65%. In some cases it went down 90%. We ended up declaring bankruptcy. We were absolute losers. I just couldn't make it work. I was really tired. And I was like, OK, my back is up against the wall. And the very next day I found Russell Branson. Yeah, where are you? Oh, I forgot about that. I just saw that part of it missing. Yeah, almost like just that image inside of there. This is a couple of pages. Like these are the best. Just coming out. How do you make these look like? Maybe that's just a funnel. How do you show like these are like, like the opt-in and then it's like. Hey, everyone, this is Russell. Hope you guys are all doing awesome. So I'm here right now in the room of, I think, 48 or 49 amazing entrepreneurs. And you guys can all see me again. And we've been talking about storytelling and like how to tell your story. And kind of the origin story of where you got started and what got you into what you're doing. So I got started in this business, I guess it's almost like 13 or 14 years ago now. And when I did, I was a college guys, I went to Boise State University, which is just down the road from right now. And I was like a wrestler. I wrestled and I had to go to school. So I barely graduated. It was like barely. Like I think my acumen level is a 2.1, which means I got a whole bunch of C's and like 1B over like five years. So it's kind of painful. But I graduated, which made my mom happy. It was pretty cool. But during school, as I was wrestling, I started learning about this internet marketing stuff. And one night, like I'm sitting on the couch and the mash ends. And then from there, it transitions into this infomercial. The second way to make money that I stumbled onto was placing tiny classified ads in the newspaper. It was this guy named Don LaPri sitting there in a beach. He's so excited, so much passion. It could make you a fortune. And he started telling the story. I remember sitting there listening to it almost like it was yesterday. I found tiny classified ads that made $30 to $40 profit in a week. He's like, and I started, I was excited. I told my friends and my family members about this ad and how much money I'd made. And they all started teasing me because like you made $30. Like that's not that big of a deal. And I placed those ads in around a thousand other newspapers around the country. He's like, but then I had the idea like, what if I took that ad that was in my local paper? And what if I put it in a thousand newspapers? That's how I generated over $50,000 a week out of my one bedroom apartment. And as soon as he said like, he said that like the light bulbs went off my head. I was like, oh my gosh, she didn't work, you know, a thousand times harder. He just replicated himself a thousand times and he made that much money. I've been doing this for the last five years and I'm still making millions of dollars. And like my hands were sweating and I was getting so excited. And I was like, God, if these guys can do it, like I'm pretty sure I can do it. So that was my goal I wanted to accomplish. Now, the other side of that I don't really share a lot of times was it wasn't just I wanted to make money. That was a desire I had, but like the bigger desires I had just gotten married. Fast forward now, 10 years later. We're back to Colette. OK. FYI, Russell didn't give me a chance to clean the house before he gave you the tour. So anyway, that said. Isn't she cute? I married her. She was working full time to support me, her jobless wrestling husband. It was his charm that and his character that drew me in. And so once we were married, then that made him start to look into how to support both of us because I did have a couple of jobs to keep us afloat while he was at school. She was working multiple jobs to pay the bills so that I could continue to wrestle and keep doing what I was doing. I don't know about you, but like I was a vision like when I got married, I'd be like the man and I would be like the supporter. And it was it was honestly pretty tough for me that she was supporting me. And I remember like I kind of figured out a way to contribute. And initially I thought maybe it's eBay. Like people make money on eBay. And I remember driving to the thrift store, buying everything that I could find that I thought was worth value. And then like on my bike with like grocery sacks full of crap, like driving my bike back home, I bought like a Michael Jackson record. So I'm like, Michael Jackson records have got to be worth a ton of money. I ended up selling it for 13 cents on eBay. So depressed. But the interesting thing that happened is like when I'm standing in the line at the post office with all these misshaped boxes and like a wheelbarrow with full of stuff, trying to like get him shipped out to my customers. And there's this dude standing there with a big, huge box full of CDs, like hundreds and hundreds of CDs. I was like, dude, like what was that? He's like, oh, I sell information products. But what does that mean? He's like, all these CDs have info burnt on people paying me. I shipped him a CD. I'm like, these are the same CDs. Like, yeah, I have a CD burner. I just burn them. And then I put them in these things and I send them out. He's like getting different boxes. He's got one thing he sells it over and over and over again. I was like, that guy's got the answer. Potato guns. Do it yourself. Internet site and Greco Roman wrestling all have in common. My very first product I ever created was a DVD on how to make potato guns. But I mean. Credit board. That was awesome for Russell Brunson's success. He and a friend thought it would be fun to go online and find out how to make a potato gun. And I searched and was able to find out that there were about 18,000 people every month searching for information about potato guns. 18,000. And there wasn't a single product out there. How much money did you end up making? My senior year, I made about $250,000. This year we did about 3 million. I probably had four or five months of success and then Google change their algorithm and it got really, really hard. One of my friends called me. This is Mike Philson. Some of you guys may know Mike and he said, he said, Russell, I figured out like how to make this whole internet marketing game work again. He's like, well, I went back to all my sites and I added these things. He called him OTO. I'm like, what's an OTO? He said, OTO stands for one time offers. Like it's like an upsell. Like when you go to McDonald's and you come to the drive-through and they ask you, hey, do you want fries with that? He's like, that's an upsell. He's like, so make these special one time offer upsell pages where someone buys the thing and then we make an offer for something better on the next page. So I remember jumping online. I was Googling like potato guns, trying to figure out what would my upsell be. I don't know. All I had was a potato gun DVD. I'm like, well, next thing you need is I have to drive home deeper and buy the pipes and the guns and all this stuff. Right. And I'm like, that's the biggest reason most people do it is like, that's like the frustrating part. What if I buy the wrong pipe? So if I come too long or too short, I get the wrong ratio, like all those, those things and he's like, you should upsell them with kit with all the stuff already finished for him. And so I went back to the drawing board. I made my first upsell. So I bought my DVD for $27 to $37 and they would buy a potato gun kit for like $200. And I think I was paying like 60 bucks for the kit or something like that. If someone bought that, I'd make $140. Turn the Google ads on and sure enough, I kept losing money when someone bought a DVD, but then one out of like three people would buy the kit, which meant I put $140 cash in my pocket, which now made the whole company profitable. And we didn't know what to call it back then. But that really, for me, that was the very first time we made a funnel or we're taking somebody through a process. And so I had this epiphany like, this is the future. Like if I can make more funnels, like this one will make me like, I don't know, 20, 30 bucks a day. But what if I had two funnels or three or five or 10 something like the playground workout, a better posture guaranteed. I'm going to show you guys some pictures of all this stuff I tried before quick funnels. In case any of you guys are wondering like, when's my funnel going to work? Q Bono, we did dating, we did weight loss, we did network marketing. You might need to do one or two or three or four a whole bunch. The harder thing for me was like, it was super techie. And I'm not a techie person at all. It would take days, sometimes weeks, going back and forth with the web guide in India and everywhere, trying to get them to just move an image and make it a little bit bigger or like things that like were so common sense, but I couldn't actually do it. It drove me nuts. They had infinite number of ideas, things they wanted to do. But if they had to wait months every time they were implementing a new idea, it just slowed them down. So they started to look online for solutions that would allow them to just move faster. You know, it's I think there's a lot of trial and error. I mean, I would like to say that we're geniuses and we just knew what it would be, but we felt the pain of the customer because we had been there. During that process, I was running basically the entire technology side of the business. I was building the sites. I was doing the e-commerce side of things. We were building custom platforms for email. After doing it for a couple of years, we're working on a project and it came up in the conversation that it would be really nice if Russell had a way to actually modify some of these things on his own. And I felt like because we had found such a specific way that really worked to sell online, that it would be possible to build a platform that actually sold things in that specific sales formal method and provided to ourselves and to other people and kind of streamline what we were doing. Russell was sitting there talking to Todd and he said, you know what, we're going to do that. What I'd really love to do. And he started coming up with this huge laundry list. They've got out the whiteboards and pens and just started going to town as far as if you were to create something for me, I would like this and I would like this and I would like this. And Todd said, you know what, I'm going to take this back tonight and I'm going to see what I can do. And the week was over. He's like, okay, what should we call this thing? And we didn't know when we were trying to figure out ideas and things. And one of the names we threw out was ClickFunnels. And right that's kind of cool. We looked for the domain that was available. And the day that we had the idea for ClickFunnels, we sat in a room for about a week whiteboarding out the whole vision of it. And on the way, taking him back to the airport, he was flying back home where he lives in Atlanta. He said, hey, if we're going to do this whole ClickFunnels thing, I don't want to be like an employee, I want to be your partner. I remember being so scared like, I don't know if I, like I'm an entrepreneur, I control everything all the time. I was so scared. And in that moment, I made the second greatest decision in my life outside of marrying my wife. And I told him, yes. And that man is Todd Dickerson, who's my co-founder and partner at ClickFunnels. Let's give Todd a huge round of applause. What's up? Oh, man. Wow. What's up, motherfuckers? Woo! I remember sitting in school a lot of times, listening to my teachers and trying to like regurgitate the stuff they tell me. I remember thinking that I was dumb. I look out at the playground and look at people playing and just wish I could be out there doing other things. And it was frustrating. I think this society as a whole doesn't know what to do with entrepreneurs. We fit in this weird box where it's like, they're creating stuff, but they're not paying attention, not sitting behind their desk, they're not following the rules. You think about life and entrepreneurship. What we're talking about, keeping the rules is about breaking the rules, about discovering and creating. When we started creating ClickFunnels, that was the initial goal. The goal was how do we create something that can free all entrepreneurs, it can break the handcuffs off. So an entrepreneur who's got the vision, who's dreaming and wants to actually create, can actually create, can actually do the thing that happened to hire 10, 15, 20 people to make their vision become a reality. The greatest opportunity that ClickFunnels provides to people who are just getting into the business world is the ability to literally get something up and running extremely fast, testing right away. Too often, a lot of great ideas and things never get actually out to the market because everyone's trying to learn, how do I actually create a website? How do I actually write copy? How do I do video? Instead of spending your time editing and doing all that kind of stuff, one of the greatest marketing blessings that ClickFunnels provides is you don't need to do all that. You can literally just go ahead and create something real quick and you can outsource the people exactly how of all your products as well as some of the additional marketing tools and services you need. I was resistant at first because of how many years I had spent trying to figure out how much money I had spent to link up Infusionsoft as my email provider, linked up inside of lead pages and Kajabi is using lead pages for my funnels, Kajabi for my membership sites, Infusionsoft for my emailing, using Stripe for my merchanting, and then using Who Knows and I remember God Knows Who calendar system and all of this was kind of in one and then we had our CRM because Infusionsoft was too confusing and so I was using trying to use Basecamp and we were putting all this together and I was like, oh my God, I cannot do this. Oh my gosh, I am not tech savvy at all and I did it all myself. I literally did it myself on my couch, seven months pregnant. I mean, I see grandma's making funnels. I was like, it can't possibly be all these things because I have no how much work it took to do this. Not knowing the software, I went to FunnelHacking live in February, learned the process in the next few months, wrote the webinar, did it all myself, launched it May 17th, had the baby May 21st. It's kind of like those are still going to be true, but you're like, holy shit, it's true and they're like, wow, it's true. The lights turned on, right? I'm like, oh my goodness, within 24 hours of launching my first funnel, I was able to close a deal that again, like surpassed what I had made in an entire year. We had done the entire previous year. We had done less than $100,000 in sales and since I launched the webinar until the end of the year, we did $280,000 just in three months. There's four different sets of people on the world. There's the caretakers of people who like taking care of other people. The question for that is, do you like changing bedpans? Do you? Not a chance, but there's people out there who like actually really like it and I've talked to them and they're not lying. The second set of people, the communicators, they're the ones that carry on oral tradition. They like to talk to each other. Do you like small talk? Communicators love it. Like you put two communicators that are water cooler, they'll spend 45 minutes talking about a half hour TV show. The third group of people is the coordinators. These are like the memorizers, the organizers, the fine print, the details, all that stuff. If you look at our evolutionary human tribe, and we go back 10,000 years and we say, there's the people who took care of other people. There's the people who communicated. There's the people who coordinated what's missing, the hunters. And when you look at the entrepreneur, the entrepreneur is an evolutionary hunter. You look at all of our sensitivities and all the things that make us weak in today's society and strong, and it's all hunter characteristics. We're highly sensitive, driven to move forward, driven for momentum, totally open to vulnerability and exposure. Like we do things that the normal human being would never do. The average person's biggest fear is public speaking. They fear public speaking more than death. Like entrepreneurs will do anything to get to their destination. I want to help you understand who you are. See, the entrepreneurial personality type has been around since the beginning of time. We are that small percentage of the population who has always heard things like sit down, slow down, stop asking so many questions and making everyone else uncomfortable. Who's heard it? Yeah. And here's who we are. See, we are driven to change the world, to create new, to make things happen. And there are three awakenings of the entrepreneurial personality type that I want to see if anyone here has. I want you to self-qualify yourself as an EPT. Every great person in history went through the three awakenings of the entrepreneurial personality type. Three awakenings about who they are. I want to see if you have them. See, the first one is, every great person in history knew they were different. They knew they were different from the people around them. They knew that they weren't the same as everybody else. How many of you knew and know now you're different, not from the people in this room, but from the crowd out there? How many? All right, we're doing good so far. The second awakening of the entrepreneurial personality type is called innate motivation. It's that thing that happens where the flip switch is on and you can't turn it off. How many people in this room can turn it off? Can you? There's only usually one. So how many of you can't turn it off and don't know why anyone would want to? All right, now the third one is big. The third one is the call of contribution. It's that thing in our head, that crazy voice that says, you're meant to do more. You're meant to be more. You're meant to leave more behind. Give more here. How many of you are hearing the call of contribution? Yeah, you can see entrepreneurial personality types were put on this planet for one reason, to change the world. I always felt like I was completely different from everyone around me. I sat down to write a book and I wrote in a weekend over 25,000 words. And that initial writing became a book called the entrepreneurial personality type. I've known Russell since 2013. I was having a hard time getting traction on anything. And from the month I talked to Russell to a year later, we were doing over a million dollars in recurring and that were it over two. Like they kept saying that I was like, this is cool. Like they've, they've got a name for themselves. And they'd heard me and Todd talk about these little hackathons we used to do. Like we used to do these all night hackathons, trying to get stuff done. And like, let's do a hackathon. And so we get these little groups around the country, people doing like two and three person meetup hackathons. I was like, Hey, this is so cool. Hey, my name is Russell Brunson. I want to welcome you to the ClickFunnels 2.com Club. Now, what is the 2.com Club? How can you become a member? And how can you win these amazing trophies on your wall? Well, before I explain exactly what the 2.com Club is and how you can become a member, I want to tell you a really quick story that happened to me a little over... I was growing up, I used to love to watch the award shows, right? We watched the Olympics. You could see the athletes run or swim or Russell, whatever their sport is. In the end, they get medals, they get gold medals, silver medals, things like that. All the industries, there's an award show except for entrepreneurship. There's no award for being an entrepreneur. I remember Russell reaching out to me, Dave, how many people do we have who have made over a million dollars with a sales funnel? Thinking at the time, it would be, you know, three or four. And I... The actual number, I forget the actual number, was over 25 or 30 people. And Russell was like, oh my gosh, we've had over 25 or 30 people who have made over a million dollars using our software. It's just, you know, we need to actually create an award or something for that. And so what we created at that time is what we refer to as the Two Comma Club. So there's two commas and a million dollars, and that was the significance behind it. What if we turn this into the Oscars or the Emmys of Entrepreneurship? And that's really where the Two Comma Club began. We want to create something where we can reward entrepreneurs for all of the work and the effort that the blood sweat tears that goes in every single day to create amazing businesses. A lot of times, they don't get credit for it. You see the business, you see the output. A lot of times, the entrepreneur doesn't get recognized. And so we want to create something that recognizes the entrepreneur. Where once a year, they can gather together among their peers and get on stage and be able to get something that shows, yes, I accomplished my goal. And if you look at most of our Two Comma Club success stories, probably all of the Two Comma Club success stories, they're drastically creating value in their industry. They're changing people's lives. They are helping people getting shaped. They're helping people with their personal lives. They're helping people with their businesses. They're helping people with real estate. They're doing all kinds of things. They're really high, high value offerings. And that's, I think, the big difference, I see. We earned it on the day of our 15-year wedding anniversary. So it was huge, crossing the stage. And I remember getting off the stage and like, burying my face in Nathan's neck and just bawling because it was like, all these years. And here we are. I can't believe we did it. When God is in the conversation, transformation can happen. Because at the pit to the peak, it's not possible to get to this peak staying the same human being you are in the pit. That's why when Russell says improvement offers don't work, it's because it goes against the grain of divine truth. We don't want improvements, we want transformation. This is why we're inspired by people's physical transformations. We're inspired by their financial transformations. We're like, wow, here's where they were. Here's where they are. Wow, I'm kind of there. I want to go there. How do I do it? And this is the magic question that sits in between these two points. I am hunting to get people to ask the question. What is the question? How? How? How do I get me some of that? How do I get a marriage like that? How do we get a body like that? How do I get a family like that? How do I make money like that? How do I feel peaceful like that? What is it that they're talking about? The peak. And so we spend our time not mastering the conversation of the peak. You will spend less than 20% of your energy talking about the peak. Your money is found in walking in the darkness with your people. This art of seduction requires you to understand that you are the light in the night. I sold and sell to men who never buy personal development. They don't buy spiritual development. I trained thousands of men to now read the Bible every day, which is wild because I wasn't reading the Bible either until about a year and a half ago. And people were like, how are you getting all these cavemen to read the Bible? I was like, it's easy. I lead them from what I live. So here's our challenge. I cannot tell you the number of supposed bajillion followers on TikTok and Instagram who show up in my house, my wife and I, and they ask me this question. How did you get this house? Where was I? Shamrock Estates. In 140 hours of our house, rental, barely paying my bills with a guy across the street, literally named Bobby. And this is good because in his garage was wrapped with NASCAR all around the garage. Bobby would come home at five o'clock every night, sit in the driveway, watch NASCAR reruns, and drink Bud Light. In Warrior, it's, is Garrett gonna do what he says he ought to do? Do I trust Garrett? But the hardest one is number four, which is do you trust yourself? That's the four story. And it's the most important story. Most people don't buy in front of a four, because they're scared about the truth inside their own lives about where they are, and they're scared about the truth of where they want to go. See, many of you are terrified more of being successful by moving forward with us here than you are worried about failing. Do you want to why? Because to be successful is gonna completely transform your entire world. Most of the people that you walk with won't walk with you anymore. Most of the people you call friends right now will not be your friends within 12 months. Most of the family members you think are so close will become some of the greatest people that attack you. Most people don't move forward into success because they're terrified of letting go of the life they have currently. Most overweight people stay overweight because they're terrified about leaving their life as they know it, because they've built a life around being this. What we're fundamentally dealing with at Convert Mindset is the shattering of one's truth and reality. I have to smash your truth. In this stage, I have to collide with what you call true about yourself. I have to expose your box as lies. I have to show you that what you have tolerated is unacceptable, that where you have tolerated your life to be is unacceptable. And in that place, paying a vision for a life that is bigger than the life that you currently have. And I've got to get you to let go of this world and reach for this one. And this process is terrifying because this is the process of death and rebirth. Yeah. You know what we're gonna ask you to do today? We're gonna ask all of you to die. I'm gonna ask you to die. Russell's gonna ask you to die tonight. Just like he asked me to die. Just like he asked my wife to die. Die to our stories about who we are and give ourselves a chance to be reborn as new beings. This is what this is. And it's all of the tactical things you're hearing. That's what this is. This is an invitation for you to die and be reborn. The crazy part is your mind's gonna think it's a sacrifice. It's not. A sacrifice would make it feel the following that I was giving up something of greater value for something of lesser value. That's sacrifice. Because you had something great and now you got something less great. I promise you right now, there was no sacrifice in leaving Shamarca States. None. I've not sacrificed one time. My life gets better every time, but see, these principles of letting go are incredibly challenging. But they're biblical. And they're true. You're all being called onto a journey. That journey is simple. When Abraham asked God for things, it was a really simple request. I want a son. I want this, God said, fine. I'll give you more. You will have more children to your name than the stars and fill this guy. Here's how it's gonna begin, my son. First, leave your country. Second, leave your family. Third, leave your father's house. This is what you're asking people to do in Convert Mindset. You're asking them to die. God was simple, leave your country, Abraham. Move from the world you currently believe in. All of you are living in a country right now called your world. You came to this place and I'm here talking to you now in a different way than you've been spoken to this whole time. Because you're now stepping into the box of decision. Am I gonna leave this place and go back to the life that I have lived in and tolerate it in or will I choose to leave my country? But then it gets scary. Second request God made. Leave your family. What does this mean? I want you to consider this means what? It means the following. The way you currently hold your family in the container of reality, your spouse, your children, your family, your life, it is constrained by your fears and your doubts and your worries. Leaving your country means leaving the story you have about yourself. Leaving your family means dying to the stories you have about your spouse and about your children and about what your family deserves. Third one, leave your father's house. I left the religion I was raised in. I left the poverty my parents had taught me. I left the scarcity I was only new. I defied the logic of everything I was raised in. And yet here I am, the one that my family leans on now, the one that was persecuted for leaving, beaten and spit upon for my decisions, looked at as evil and wicked, and yet the one person in the entire family making the greatest impact on humanity. And I get it, because it's scary. My mom said in a marriage with my father, she probably should have left 30 years ago. My mom had a story. I must stay home and take care of the children. My mom was 10 times the producer my dad was. So my poor father went out there with popsicle sticks trying to fight and protect the family. And he was getting slaughtered every day. My mom is a savage, hiding swords in her pockets and pretending she didn't have them, cutting herself and cutting us and cutting my dad. My mom lived in a lie and now she's in her 70s. And it's sad. It's sad. It's sad to me to see people just choose to die. Most of my family's obese. Like, why? Why? See, it's gonna hurt no matter what. It's gonna hurt. If you stay where you are, it's gonna hurt. It's gonna hurt. Play it forward five years from now. You leave this place. You don't come with us. You leave this place. Play it out. Is life gonna get better? Stay the same. Or get worse for you economically. Type in the chat what you think will happen. I'm not saying you're weak. I'm saying there's consequences for decisions. So type it in the chat. You don't have forever. And when my wife and I came to terms with this, and yeah. I've had cancer. Rock of fucking cancer. I've been told I was gonna die four or five different times. But see, once you've been in that pocket, you're like, eh, it's the worst thing that happens, I die. Okay, what's better than that? Anything else? Okay, cool. So I mean, people are gonna run their mouths about me on social media? Eh, that's not real bad. Oh, what do I need to do? I need to make an investment in myself of time, energy, and money so that I can move forward to the pursue the life I want. You don't have forever. I've never met one person that came to this place like you. Who said, man, I'm sure glad I waited a couple years. Like just feeling really good about the fact that I waited a couple years. You know, the worst part is, is when people watch my life continue to get so big, and they continue to sit on the sidelines and not do anything. I have guys in my world right now who've come back into my world in the last three years. They're like, coach, is this like at this point? I don't even know what to say. It's like, your life is getting so big. I was like, the body thing you did, it's just put me over the top now. Like, what am I doing? I was like, I don't know what you're doing. But I've told you I'm gonna do the same thing I've always done. I'm gonna identify a place God's calling me to, and I'm gonna go. I don't care if you go. Because I don't live your life. I don't marry your wife. I'm not raising your children. That's on you. See, the last couple of days we've been working with you to help you see there's this place, this place that you're in. And if you could see what's waiting for you over here, even if your life is great right now, this place over here that we've all pursued and are pursuing here in this community, that place will make your current reality disgusting to you. Just three years ago, the version of me, I'm disgusted by that guy. Two years ago, I'm disgusted by that guy. The man I am right now would devour the guy I was a year ago. Like, destroy him. Why? Because this game never ends. Retirement is a lie sold you by liars. God said, rest, not retire. Lies everywhere. The biggest lies are the lies that you tell yourself. The saddest one is, you're not worth being here. You know that's the hardest part of story for it. It's helping people see that they're worth it. I wish there was a way I could just reach into your world and grab the version of you two years from now and just bring that person back and put them at a table with you. And they could talk to you. And say, come on. Don't keep us stuck here. Let's go. This version of me has always been here. Always. I just had to dig them out of the prison I'd put them in. I put them there. Nobody else did.
